LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE RECORD SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A TIMELY OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. THIS IS A BASIS TO DENY THE MOTION. HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER THE BRIEF AND ADDRESS THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A STATUTORY BASIS FOR HER CLAIMS AGAINST PUBLIC ENTITY DEFENDANT. (SEE EASTBURN V. REGIONAL FIRE PROTECTION AUTHORITY (2003) 31 CAL.4TH 1175, 1183; ZELIG V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 1112, 1132.) PLAINTIFF MAY FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 15 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF THE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2-4 (ABUSE OF POWER, UNDUE INFLUENCE, AND FRAUD). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE A STATUTORY BASIS FOR THOSE CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT, WHO IS A PUBLIC ENTITY. SEE EASTBURN V. REGIONAL FIRE PROTECTION AUTHORITY (2003) 31 CAL.4TH 1175, 1183; ZELIG V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 1112, 1132. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT HER OSTEOPENIA IS A DISABILITY OR HOW THE CART ACCOMMODATES THAT CONDITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF TRUFANT, CAROL A. TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CAROL TRUFANT, ESQ., 510.221.4504, PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
ELIZABETH PEDERSON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 2-4 (abuse of power, undue influence, and fraud). Plaintiff fails to allege a statutory basis for those claims against Defendant, who is a public entity. See Eastburn v. Regional Fire Protection Authority (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1175, 1183; Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1132. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to cause of action 1. Plaintiff does not allege that her Osteopenia is a disability or how the cart accommodates that condition. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Continued To Feb-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Nov-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DR. CAROL TRUFANT, TEL. NO. (510) 221-4504, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. ELIZABETH PEDERSON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3862 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is granted with 15 days leave to amend. The record shows that Plaintiff failed to file a timely opposition to the motion. This is a basis to deny the motion. However, the Court will consider the brief and address the merits. Plaintiff has failed to allege a statutory basis for her claims against public entity Defendant. (See Eastburn v. Regional Fire Protection Authority (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1175, 1183; Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1132.) Plaintiff may file an Amended Complaint within 15 days after entry of the order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
